# OpenVent

> Open-core, self-hosted voice AI orchestration framework. Bring your own Twilio, Deepgram,
> ElevenLabs/Cartesia, and LLM keys — OpenVent wires them into one live pipeline while you own
> the code, the database, and the data.

OpenVent sits deliberately between two extremes: fully local voice AI (your own GPU running a
local LLM/STT/TTS — real, but a different product with real latency/quality tradeoffs) and fully
managed platforms (Vapi, Retell — zero infrastructure, zero control). OpenVent self-hosts the
orchestration layer — call logic, state engine, compliance rules, dashboard — and lets you bring
your own AI/telephony providers, instead of chasing either extreme.

## What it does

- Inbound and outbound voice calls over Twilio, with real-time speech-to-text (Deepgram) and
  text-to-speech (ElevenLabs or Cartesia), and an LLM agent in between (AI Gateway or Groq)
- Barge-in: the caller can interrupt the agent mid-sentence and the agent stops instantly
- Tool calling mid-conversation (CRM sync to GoHighLevel/Salesforce/HubSpot, Google Calendar
  booking), wrapped in a shared timeout/retry/circuit-breaker layer
- A structured state engine — the agent captures durable facts (name, email, order ID) instead of
  re-deriving them from conversation scrollback every turn
- Compliance enforced automatically: TCPA calling-window restrictions, Do-Not-Call list checks
  before every outbound call, spoken AI/recording disclosure by default, a HIPAA boot guardrail,
  and GDPR-style data retention purge plus right-to-erasure
- A compliance audit-trail export — per call or per phone number, as plain text or JSON, showing
  who was called, when, under what consent, and what was actually said
- An operator dashboard for calls, transcripts, captured state, and Do-Not-Call management
- Outgoing webhooks compatible with n8n, Zapier, and Make
- Every provider (LLM, TTS, STT) is swappable behind a provider abstraction — no code changes

## What it is not

- Not a fully managed platform. There is no OpenVent-hosted phone number or dashboard you sign up
  for without running the code yourself (yet — a managed hosted tier is a possible future paid
  layer on top of the free self-hosted core, not built today).
- Not fully local. Telephony (PSTN via Twilio) and AI model inference (STT/LLM/TTS) remain cloud
  APIs by necessity — no one self-hosts a phone network, and running frontier-quality models on
  your own hardware is a different product with different tradeoffs.
- Not yet at feature or scale parity with funded competitors like Vapi, Retell, or Bland — OpenVent
  is younger and openly says so, betting instead on transparency and self-host verifiability.

## License

Fair-code — the OpenVent Sustainable Use License. Free to self-host, modify, and use for internal
or personal purposes forever. Not licensed for repackaging as a competing hosted/managed service
without a separate commercial license.

## Links

- Homepage: https://openvent.dev/
- Documentation: https://openvent.dev/docs
- Source code: https://github.com/I-invincib1e/vent
- Standalone compliance package (usable outside OpenVent): @openvent/compliance
